nn6_sr_benchmark.summary: 10 SRBench functions evaluated for EML vs PySR/gplearn advantage

eml_wins.pattern: All transcendental targets (ln, exp, trig via ceml/deml)

eml_loses.pattern: All polynomial targets — add_gen=11n penalises sums heavily

eml_neutral.pattern: Rational/combinatorial — no structural EML advantage or disadvantage

nn6_sr_benchmark.key_rule: EML wins iff target is transcendental (exp, ln, trig). Loses on polynomials.

nn7_activation_shootout.headline: softplus = LEAd(x,1) = 1n — the cheapest possible EML activation
nn7_activation_shootout.pinn_insight: Physics equations use exp and ln natively. softplus networks ARE EML transformations layer-by-layer. Distillation recovers the physics equation exactly.
nn7_activation_shootout.classification_winner: ReLU — dying neurons survivable with SGD+BN; fastest training
nn7_activation_shootout.eml_distillation_winner: softplus — 1n cost, no dead neurons, exact distillation
nn7_activation_shootout.gradient_wall_fix: DEML damps exp explosion at deep layers; LEAd bounds gradient via sigmoid

sin_x.failure_reason: Infinite zeros barrier — EML expressions have only finitely many real zeros

ReLU.failure_reason: Smoothness barrier — EML expressions are C-∞; ReLU has a kink at x=0

nn8_distillation.completeness_rule: EML complete for {exp, ln, *, /, -, +, recip} compositions. Incomplete for trig and piecewise.

sweet_spot_rationale: Gradient stability HIGH: softplus gradient ∈ (0,1), DEML damps explosion
Covers: Arrhenius, sigmoid, log-linear, exponential decay, Gaussian envelopes

when_to_extend.add_add_gen: when polynomial targets mandatory; accept gradient instability

sections: Hybrid architecture: DNN trunk + EML symbolic head
D=4 gradient wall and how DEML/LEAd solve it
SR benchmark: EML wins on transcendental, loses on polynomial
Distillation: success on Arrhenius/sigmoid, failure on sin/ReLU
Honest conclusion: specialised PINN tool, not general SR

cross_session_findings.primary_result: EML symbolic heads are a specialised tool for physics-informed ML, not general SR
cross_session_findings.win_condition: Target is built from {exp, ln, trig (ceml)} — transcendental functions
cross_session_findings.lose_condition: Target is polynomial — add_gen=11n makes sums brutally expensive
cross_session_findings.fail_condition: Target is piecewise (ReLU) or periodic (sin) — EML completeness theorem
cross_session_findings.best_activation: softplus = LEAd = 1n — the only activation that IS an EML primitive
cross_session_findings.best_operator_subset: {EML, DEML, LEAd} — gradient stable, 1n cost, 90-95% distillation success
cross_session_findings.compression_ratio: ~100:1 for Arrhenius-class physics targets (500 params → 5 EML nodes)
cross_session_findings.gradient_wall_fix: DEML for exp dampening; LEAd (softplus) for bounded gradient via sigmoid
